skill.name: maxhub-tiktok
skill.description: TikTok data assistant for querying MaxHub API endpoints covering videos, users, search, ads, creators, shops, and interactions.
skill.summary: Query and analyze TikTok data through documented MaxHub API workflows.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: Static findings are partly false positives from Markdown code spans and reference tables, but the skill intentionally runs curl against a third-party API with MAXHUB_API_KEY. Human review is required because references include metric manipulation and interaction capabilities that conflict with the stated read-only claim.

high_findings.title: Third-Party Credential Transmission
high_findings.description: The skill requires MAXHUB_API_KEY and instructs curl requests that send it as an Authorization bearer token to https://www.aconfig.cn. This is expected for the service, but it exposes user credentials to a community third-party API provider.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key requirement, network destination, and Authorization header are all explicitly documented. The risk is confirmed, although it may be legitimate for this API wrapper.

high_findings.title: Metric Manipulation Endpoint Documented
high_findings.description: The skill claims it is read-only, but a referenced endpoint is described as increasing a TikTok video play count by video ID. This creates abuse risk and contradicts the stated marketplace safety boundary.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The read-only claim and the metric-increase description are both direct evidence. The endpoint description is specific enough to confirm semantic risk.

high_findings.title: Interaction and Token Tooling Require Review
high_findings.description: README lists interaction actions such as like, follow, post_comment, reply_comment, collect, and forward. The skill also routes token, device, signature, and guest-cookie tooling, which may support account automation or access control bypass.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interaction and token-tool names are explicitly present, but not every endpoint implementation was verified as write-capable. The combination is strong enough for high-risk human review.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution Is Mostly Documented Usage
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many backtick execution hits, but most are Markdown code spans or fenced examples. The confirmed command surface is curl and environment checks, with no evidence found of user-controlled shell interpolation beyond documented endpoint parameters.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct shell snippets exist, but the huge static count is inflated by Markdown formatting. I did not find evidence of hidden Ruby execution in the inspected context.

medium_findings.title: Platform Signature and Device Utilities
medium_findings.description: References describe TikTok request encryption, signature, token, web ID, and device registration utilities. These can be legitimate API helpers, but they may also facilitate scraping or automation that bypasses normal client controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint descriptions and routing table explicitly include signature, token, and device tooling. Intent could be legitimate analytics support, so the confidence is high but not conclusive.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Expected Service References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to the declared MaxHub service and public TikTok media URLs in documentation. These are expected for an API reference skill and are not by themselves suspicious.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs align with the advertised service and documentation examples. The risk comes from credential transmission and endpoint behavior, not the presence of static URLs.

low_findings.title: High Entropy and Long Line Look Like Generated Documentation
low_findings.description: The high entropy and long-line heuristics correspond to large generated Markdown reference tables and route lists. No evidence found of binary payloads or encoded executable content in the inspected sections.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The long line is a visible endpoint routing table, and the referenced files are Markdown API documentation. This supports a false-positive assessment for obfuscation, while preserving the separate behavior risks.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Requests With Bearer Credentials
dangerous_patterns.description: curl examples send MAXHUB_API_KEY in an Authorization bearer header to a third-party domain.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact header construction and network destination are documented in adjacent lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Metric Increase Capability
dangerous_patterns.description: A reference endpoint description says it increases a TikTok video play count by video ID.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint purpose is directly stated in the reference text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential and Cookie Handling Warning
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill warns that some endpoints require platform session cookies and that credentials are sent to the third-party service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The privacy warning confirms sensitive credential and cookie handling in normal use.

content.user_title: Analyze TikTok Data with MaxHub APIs
content.value_statement: TikTok research often requires many separate endpoints and careful parameter choices. This skill routes requests and formats results for video, user, search, ads, and shop analysis.
actual_capabilities: Checks for a configured MAXHUB_API_KEY before making API requests.
Builds GET and POST curl requests for MaxHub TikTok API endpoints.
Routes user intent across video, user, search, ads, creator, shop, interaction, and tool references.
Supports English and Chinese response patterns with localized number formatting.
Provides analysis and comparison flows for creators, ads, products, trends, and comments.
Documents error handling for common API responses such as 401, 403, 429, and empty results.
limitations: Requires a MaxHub API key and network access to https://www.aconfig.cn.
Sends API credentials and request data to a third-party service provider.
Some endpoints may require TikTok session cookies or special permissions.
Marketplace reviewers should verify interaction and metric-related endpoints before publication.

use_cases.title: Evaluate creator partnerships
use_cases.description: Compare creator profiles, videos, audience signals, and performance metrics before outreach.

use_cases.title: Research TikTok ad trends
use_cases.description: Search ads, inspect creative patterns, and summarize engagement signals for campaign planning.

use_cases.title: Track product and shop demand
use_cases.description: Review product lists, shop data, reviews, and regional trend signals for ecommerce decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Find a TikTok video
prompt_templates.prompt: Find details for this TikTok video URL and summarize views, likes, comments, author, and publish time.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a creator
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the TikTok creator @username. Include profile data, recent video performance, audience signals, and partnership risks.

prompt_templates.title: Compare ad examples
prompt_templates.prompt: Search TikTok ads for this product category in the United States. Compare the top results by creative angle and engagement.

prompt_templates.title: Build a regional trend report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a TikTok trend report for this niche across three regions. Combine search, video, creator, product, and ad signals.

output_examples.output: Creator overview with follower count, recent video themes, engagement range, audience fit, content risks, and a recommendation.

output_examples.input: Find trending TikTok products for beauty in the United States.

output_examples.output: Side-by-side summary of ad hooks, product claims, audience signals, creative formats, and likely testing opportunities.

best_practices: Use scoped API credentials and rotate them after sensitive research.
Start with read-only lookup and analysis endpoints before using advanced tools.
Record region, date range, and endpoint limits in every report.
anti_patterns: Do not paste full browser cookies unless you trust the third-party service.
Do not use metric manipulation or interaction endpoints without explicit authorization.
Do not treat third-party API output as verified platform truth.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires MAXHUB_API_KEY for MaxHub API requests.

faq.question: Where are requests sent?
faq.answer: Requests are sent to https://www.aconfig.cn, the MaxHub API service.

faq.question: Can it analyze creators and ads?
faq.answer: Yes. The references cover creator, ad, search, video, product, and shop workflows.

faq.question: Is it read-only?
faq.answer: The skill claims read-only use, but references include interaction and metric-related capabilities that need review.

faq.question: Does it support Chinese and English?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes response rules for both Chinese and English users.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publishing?
faq.answer: Not without human review. Credential handling, third-party requests, and possible interaction endpoints create high risk.
